page: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, which map to 2 physical cores utilizing hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and 45 MB L3 cache. It supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

In benchmark evaluations, the m4.xlarge demonstrates performance characteristics that reflect its older hardware generation. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also fall into the bottom 25% category. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations show lower-tier performance, landing in the bottom 10%. However, the instance achieves an average rating in the middle 50% for LLM text generation using a small 135M model, processing 71.79 tokens per second.

The instance does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Given the dedicated CPU allocation but overall weak benchmark scores, the m4.xlarge is positioned as a legacy general-purpose option. It is suitable for low-intensity workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale database operations, and lightweight background utility tasks that require a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio without demanding high computational throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz on the Xen hypervisor. The x86_64 architecture includes 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 45 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks consistently place the m4.xlarge in the bottom 25% for single-core and multi-core CPU tasks, as well as memory bandwidth. It achieves average performance for text generation with small language models, processing 71.79 tokens per second. Given its older hardware profile and lower performance tier, this instance is best utilized for legacy general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and low-intensity utility applications that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high computational or memory throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, suitable for legacy workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for basic legacy workloads.
tagline: A legacy general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for low-intensity workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Xen hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Benchmark results place CPU and memory performance in the bottom 25%
Suitable for basic web serving, small databases, and legacy utility tasks
